---
title: "Student Mental Health & Study Habits"
subtitle: "ETC5513 Assignment 3 — Group 2"
author: "Group 2 Members"
date: "Semester 1, 2026"
bibliography: references.bib
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: simple
    slide-number: true
    transition: fade
    footer: "ETC5513 Assignment 3 | Group 2"
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

## Problem Introduction

- 60-70% of university students experience moderate to severe stress [@acha2023]
- Poor mental health linked to lower GPA and higher dropout rates
- Identifying modifiable factors enables targeted interventions

Research question: How do sleep quality, study habits, and counseling access relate to student stress and anxiety?

---

## Dataset Description

- Sample: 1,200 students
- Key variables: sleep quality, stress, anxiety, weekly study hours, GPA, and counseling access

---

## Methods

- Data cleaned in R using tidyverse and janitor [@hunt2010mental]
- Pearson correlations and t-tests applied
- Fully reproducible via Quarto and renv

---

## Results — Study Hours and GPA

```{r}
#| fig-height: 4
library(tidyverse)

student_data <- read_csv("data/processed/student_data_clean.csv",
  show_col_types = FALSE
)

ggplot(student_data, aes(x = hours_studied_per_week, y = gpa)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.35, color = "#2E86AB", size = 1.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "#A23B72", se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2) +
  labs(x = "Hours Studied per Week", y = "GPA") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
---
Results — Sleep Quality and Stress
#| fig-height: 4
student_data |>
  mutate(sleep_category = factor(sleep_category,
    levels = c("Poor", "Fair", "Good")
  )) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = sleep_category, y = stress_level, fill = sleep_category)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Poor" = "#d73027", "Fair" = "#fee090", "Good" = "#1a9850")) +
  labs(x = "Sleep Quality", y = "Stress Level (1-10)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
---
  
Key Findings
Finding	Value
Study hours vs GPA	0.52, p < 0.001
Sleep quality vs Stress	-0.48, p < 0.001
Anxiety difference with counseling	-2.1 points
Stress difference good vs poor sleep	-3.1 points
Conclusions and Recommendations

Conclusions:

Sleep quality is the strongest modifiable predictor of stress
Counseling access independently reduces anxiety by 2.1 points

Recommendations:

Students should prioritise 7-9 hours sleep nightly
Universities should expand counseling and sleep health programs
Researchers should use longitudinal studies to confirm causal pathways
References

::: {#refs}
:::
